title: Python Decorators
summary: Decorators are functions that wrap another function to extend or modify its behavior without changing its source code.
common_mistakes: Forgetting to return the wrapper function
Losing the original function's metadata (name, docstring)
practice_idea: Create a decorator that logs function calls, including their arguments and execution time.

title: Python Generators
summary: Generators let you produce a sequence of values lazily, one at a time, instead of building a full list in memory.
common_mistakes: Trying to reuse an exhausted generator
Confusing return with yield inside a generator
practice_idea: Write a generator that yields Fibonacci numbers indefinitely, then take the first 10 values.

title: Context Managers
summary: Context managers use the 'with' statement to guarantee setup and cleanup of resources, even if an error occurs.
common_mistakes: Not releasing resources when an exception occurs
Forgetting __exit__ must return a boolean to control exception suppression
practice_idea: Write a context manager that times how long a block of code takes to run.

title: Object-Oriented Inheritance
summary: Inheritance lets a class reuse and extend the attributes and methods of another class.
practice_idea: Model a Vehicle base class with Car and Bike subclasses that override a describe() method.

title: Async/Await
summary: Async/await lets Python run I/O-bound tasks concurrently without using multiple threads.
common_mistakes: Calling an async function without awaiting it
Blocking the event loop with synchronous code
practice_idea: Write an async function that fetches multiple URLs concurrently using asyncio.gather.

title: List Comprehensions
summary: A concise syntax for creating lists by transforming or filtering an existing iterable.
common_mistakes: Writing comprehensions so complex they hurt readability
Confusing comprehension scope with the enclosing scope
practice_idea: Write a list comprehension that filters even numbers from a list and squares them.

title: Error Handling with Try/Except
summary: Techniques to catch, handle, and raise exceptions to keep programs robust.
common_mistakes: Catching overly broad exceptions like Exception or bare except
practice_idea: Write a function that reads a file and handles FileNotFoundError with a custom error message.
